title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Model Lens: Place Value
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg place value increment in a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Place Value evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Place Value for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation.
steps.why: The Place Value result for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
